_note: Chapter 7 — Gentry taps the aleph through the Count. The gray house. The cost.

c7_bench.name: The Count's Corner
c7_bench.desc: A week of vigil has made the corner a ward: stretcher, drip, Cherry's shoebox telemetry pulsing amber. The Count's eyes move under their lids like weather on another planet. The gray slab is warm to the touch. You've stopped touching it.

c7_ground.name: Factory — Ground Floor
c7_ground.desc: Torchlight and machine skeletons. The Judge stands half-rebuilt where you left it, one arm raised like a question you haven't answered. Overhead, Gentry's loft burns its lonely light.

interactions.label: Name the Investigators you still remember
pages: The small machines came first. Each Investigator carries a tool for discovering what happened: probe, lamp, saw, magnet, claw. You named none of them after people because people are where the missing time lives.

One chassis bears a court-date stamp hammered flat beneath your weld. Another uses the door of a prison transport. You remember acquiring neither. The machines circle the Judge like questions around a verdict, and suddenly you understand the joke your hands made while your mind was elsewhere.

c7_generator.name: Generator Shed
c7_generator.desc: The Factory's heart: a scavenged fusion cell and a prayer of cabling. The aleph tap draws more every night. When the needles dip, somewhere inside a universe, it is dreaming harder.

interactions.label: Audit Gentry's stolen power
pages: Gentry spoofs the Fission Authority with a monthly ritual of false load reports. The Factory appears on their grid as a pumping station two counties east. Every lie has held—until the aleph arrived and consumption began climbing in pulses too regular to explain as machinery.

The latest pulse drew enough power for a town and lasted eleven seconds. Somewhere inside the slab, architecture changed. The Count's heart slowed during the same interval. Generator, body, and constructed universe are now one circuit, and only Cherry seems willing to call the fuel by its name.

c7_catwalk.name: High Catwalk
c7_catwalk.desc: Wind through broken lights, the Solitude spread black below. On the interstate, kilometers out: headlights in convoy formation, holding steady, patient. Coming here. Nobody drives to Dog Solitude by accident.

c7_loft.name: Gentry's Loft — the Rig
c7_loft.desc: Every screen Gentry owns is turned to the tap like flowers to a black sun. He's strung a second trode set — yours. 'You hold the line,' he says, eyes enormous. 'I read the Shape. And Slick — whatever speaks in there, we do NOT insult it.'

interactions.label: Trace the second intelligence in the aleph
pages: Bobby's activity looks improvised: structures growing, paths revised, whole regions opened like rooms under renovation. Another signature moves through the aleph with inherited authority. It never builds. It closes, redirects, and marks ownership.

Gentry isolates a recursive seal attached to old Tessier-Ashpool credentials. The name beneath it is Lady 3Jane Marie-France. Officially dead. Administratively omnipresent. The aleph contains a young man making a world and a dead heiress insisting the world remains her property.
